title: Trade & Vendor Relations Coordinator
description:
## About us



We are real estate development company based in Canada. We design, permit, and build multi-unit residential buildings. We currently have 4 active construction sites and 20+ projects in our design pipeline. Our construction involves 10-15 different trade categories per project — concrete, framing, drywall, painting, plumbing, electrical, HVAC, tile, flooring, siding, roofing, insulation, glass, and more.



We are looking for someone to own the ongoing relationship with our network of construction trades and material suppliers. This is not a project-by-project procurement role — we have a separate tea
mber who handles bid packages and RFQs for specific projects. This role is about building and maintaining the NETWORK so that when a project needs a trade, we already have 3-5 vetted options with known pricing, availability, and reliability.



---



## What Your Week Actually Looks Like



**Monday**

- 8:00 AM (your time): Open the trade roster database. Review which contacts are flagged for follow-up this week. Prioritize by: trades we'll need for upcoming projects, contacts we haven't spoken to in 2+ weeks, and new leads to qualify.

- 9:00-12:00 PM: Make 8-10 outbound calls. These are NOT cold sales calls. These are relationship maintenance calls:

  - For existing contacts: "Hey Peter, it's [name] from Helio. Just checking in — how's your schedule looking for April? We have a couple of interior paint scopes coming up in the next few weeks and wanted to see if you'd have crew availability."

  - For new contacts: "Hi, I'm calling from Helio Urban Development. We're a residential developer in Halifax building multi-unit projects. I understand you do [trade]. I'd like to learn about your crew, your rates, and whether you'd be interested in quoting on upcoming work."

- Document every call: availability status, current rate range, crew size, upcoming availability windows, any concerns or notes.

- 1:00-3:00 PM (overlapping with Halifax): Coordinate with our procurement team. They're preparing bid packages for active projects — you provide the shortlist of 3-5 vendors per trade category based on your relationships. "For siding on 917 Richard, I'd recommend Annapolis, BentleyBuilt, and Summit. Annapolis is most responsive. Summit quoted high last time but does quality work. BentleyBuilt needs follow-up — they've been slow to respond."

- 3:00-4:00 PM: Update the vendor and trade database. Add new contacts. Update availability and rate information. Flag any reliability concerns from project feedback.



**Tuesday-Thursday:** Same rhythm. Different contacts from the roster. You're working through the full database systematically — every active contact gets touched at least once every 2-3 weeks.



Additionally:

- When a new project enters pre-construction, you source the initial trade shortlist. Our estimator provides the trade categories and estimated hours. You match those to your roster and provide 3-5 options per category.

- When we receive project outcome data (a trade completed work on a project), you update their roster profile: quality rating, timeliness, would we use them again, actual cost vs estimate.



**Friday**

- Weekly roster report: Total active contacts by trade category. New contacts added this week. Contacts who haven't responded in 30+ days (flag for removal or re-engagement). Any trade categories where we have fewer than 3 reliable options (gap alert).

- Plan next week's call priority list.



---



## What You're NOT Doing



- You are NOT sending bid packages or collecting formal quotes for specific projects. Our procurement team handles project-specific RFQs using your shortlists.

- You are NOT managing active construction sites or coordinating day-to-day contractor work. Our project coordinators handle on-site logistics.

- You are NOT negotiating contracts or approving payments. Our finance team handles contracts and payment terms.

- You ARE the person who knows every trade in our network personally — their strengths, their rates, their reliability, and their availability.



---



## What We Need From You



**Must have:**

- Experience maintaining a database of 50+ subcontractor or vendor contacts in a construction, property management, or general contracting environment. You have personally managed trade relationships — not just sent
s, but called people regularly, built rapport, and maintained a usable network.

- Comfortable making 8-10 outbound calls per day, every day. This is a phone-heavy role. You are not uncomfortable calling people you haven't spoken to in weeks and restarting the conversation naturally.

- Construction trade knowledge. You understand what drywallers, plumbers, electricians, HVAC technicians, framers, and painters do. You can have a credible 5-minute conversation about scope, crew sizing, and timelines without being a tradesperson yourself. When a plumber tells you "rough-in takes 3 days for an 8-unit building," you know whether that's reasonable.

- Organized database management. You maintain a structured system (spreadsheet, CRM, or database tool) where every contact has: trade category, contact info, rate range, crew size, last contact date, availability status, reliability notes, and project history. Nothing falls through.

- English sufficient for phone conversations with Nova Scotia tradespeople and suppliers. These are working-class Canadians — the conversations are direct, informal, and practical. You need to understand regional accents and construction terminology.



**Strongly preferred:**

- Experience working with general contractors in the Middle East, North America, or Australia — environments where subcontractor coordination across multiple trades is standard practice.

- Experience with vendor qualification and compliance (workers' comp certificates, insurance, safety certifications, business licenses). We are building a compliance tracking system and need someone who understands what documentation to collect.

- Experience with construction staffing — sourcing individual tradespeople (not just companies) for specific roles. We maintain a database of 2,400+ individual construction workers and need someone to build relationships with the most promising candidates.



---



## Screening Questions



Please answer all 5 questions in your application. Applications without answers will not be reviewed.



1. **Network scale:** In your most relevant role, how many subcontractor or vendor contacts did you personally maintain? Describe your system for tracking them. How often did you contact your key relationships when you had NO active project need for them?



2. **Scenario — Sourcing:** We need a reliable painting crew for a 21,000 sqft interior paint job on an 8-unit residential building in Windsor, Nova Scotia. Timeline: start in 1 week, complete in 10 days. You have 6 painters in your database — 2 you've worked with before (one was good but expensive, one was cheap but slow), 2 are new contacts you've only spoken to once, and 2 haven't responded to your last outreach. Walk me through your approach to filling this need.



3. **Scenario — Difficult vendor:** A supplier who has been reliable on previous projects submitted an invoice that's 30% higher than their original quote, claiming scope changes. Your project team says no scope changes were authorized. How do you handle this conversation with the vendor while preserving the relationship for future work?



4. **Scenario — Gap identification:** You're reviewing your roster and notice you only have 1 reliable HVAC installer in your database. You have 3 projects that will need HVAC work in the next 2 months. What do you do this week to close the gap?



5. **Relationship vs transaction:** What's the difference between a procurement coordinator who sends RFQs and collects quotes, and someone who manages trade relationships? Give a specific example from your experience where a relationship you built made a project outcome better than it would have been otherwise.



---



## 30-Day Proof Point



By day 30, we will know this is working if:

- The trade roster has been fully audited: every existing contact has current availability, rate range, and last-contact date

- At least 30 outbound relationship calls have been made and logged

- For each active construction project, a shortlist of 3-5 vetted trade options per remaining trade category has been provided to the procurement team

- At least 2 new trade contacts per major category (concrete, framing, drywall, painting, plumbing, electrical, HVAC) have been added to the roster from proactive sourcing

- A weekly roster report has been delivered every Friday for 4 consecutive weeks



---



## How to Apply



Reply to this posting with your answers to all 5 screening questions and your resume. Include the word "NETWORK" in your subject line so we know you read the full posting.

title: Executive Assistant
description:
WFH

Graveyard Shift



Position Overview



We are seeking a highly experienced and proactive Executive Assistant to support a fast-paced executive leader. This role requires a self-starter who can anticipate needs, manage priorities independently, and operate without constant direction.



The ideal candidate has extensive experience supporting senior executives in fast-growing organizations, demonstrates *excellent organizational skills*, and can manage *calendar scheduling, travel arrangements, and executive priorities with minimal supervision*.



This is not an entry-level EA position. The executive expects a professional who can take ownership, solve problems independently, and keep operations running smoothly.



Key Responsibilities



Executive Support



* Provide high-level administrative support to the executive.

* Manage confidential and sensitive information with discretion.

* Act as a *gatekeeper and point of contact* for internal and external stakeholders.

* Anticipate executive needs and proactively resolve issues before they arise.



Calendar Management



* Manage the executive’s complex and constantly changing calendar.

* Schedule, prioritize, and coordinate meetings *without requiring constant approval or direction*.

* Arrange meetings with internal teams and *external stakeholders independently.



 Travel Coordination



* Handle end-to-end travel arrangements, including:



  * Flight bookings

  * Hotel accommodations

  * Ground transportation

  * Detailed travel itineraries

* Ensure travel plans are *optimized for efficiency and executive productivity*.



Meeting Coordination



* Organize meetings with *clients, partners, and external stakeholders*.

* Prepare meeting agendas, documentation, and follow-ups.

* Track action items and ensure timely completion.



Operational Support



* Assist with project coordination and executive initiatives.

* Maintain clear communication across departments.

* Manage competing priorities in a high-pressure environment.



Candidate Requirements

Experience

* 5+ years experience as an Executive Assistant* supporting senior leadership.

* Experience working in fast-paced organizations (preferably 150+ employees).

* Proven ability to support executives who operate at a *high level of speed and demand.



Skills & Competencies



* Exceptional calendar management skills.

* Strong travel booking and logistics coordination experience.

* Excellent organizational and time management abilities.

* Strong communication and interpersonal skills.

* Ability to *ork independently without being micromanaged.

* Highly proactive and solution-oriented.



# Work Style Expectations



* The executive does not spoon-feed instructions.

* The EA must anticipate needs and act without constant guidance.

* Must be comfortable working in a high-performance, fast-moving environment.



-- Ideal Candidate Profile



The ideal Executive Assistant:

* Is well-established in the EA profession

* Is not a beginner or learning the role for the first time

* Thrives in high-expectation environments

* Takes initiative and gets things done without being asked repeatedly



Send your resume to

We’re an Australian-based NDIS provider and operations team supporting disability businesses across Australia.



We’re looking for a reliable, detail-oriented Virtual Admin Assistant with NDIS experience to support the backend operations of growing providers.



This is not a basic admin role — you’ll be part of a team that keeps services running smoothly behind the scenes.

You’ll be responsible for maintaining the day-to-day administrative flow of NDIS providers — ensuring everything is accurate, organised, and running on time.

-Takes ownership of their work

title: Research Analyst - Public Opinion
description:
Remote - Eastern Time Zone - Some weekend hours required



Job description?

***ONLY APPLY IF YOU HAVE MARKET OR OPINION RESEARCH EXPERIENCE***

**ONLY APPLY IF YOU HAVE EXCEPTIONAL ENGLISH PROOFREADING SKILLS***



Are you a passionate and detail-oriented researcher who wants to be a respected member of our team?



We are searching for candidates who:



Have exceptional English proofreading and editing skills for grammar, clarity, and style consistency



Can verify and maintain data accuracy across all stages of the research process



Have an exceptional eye for detail



Exhibit strong initiative/bias to action



Can perform tasks in a fast-paced environment



Work effectively both independently and as part of a team



More about the position:



Quality control: Review all deliverables for grammar, spelling, and formatting accuracy; cross-check data sets to ensure consistency and integrity before client delivery

Quantitative research: Develop comprehensive written reports

Data collection: Program and test surveys to ensure accuracy, sample design, and survey distribution

Data processing and analysis: Analyze research results, code and analyze open-ended responses, work with pivot tables, SPSS, R, or other software

Data visualization: Create visuals for reporting and dashboards (PowerPoint, Canva, Looker Studio, etc.)

Qualitative Research: Manage and analyze focus groups/in-depth interviews, coordinate recruiting, assist in reporting

Participate in client meetings by taking notes, identifying key action steps, understanding client needs, and making presentations



Required qualifications:



At least one year of market or opinion research experience

A bachelor’s degree or higher

Proven English proofreading and editing ability

Experience with data quality checks and error detection in research outputs

Strong communication skills



What we provide:



A supportive, inclusive, and collaborative work environment

Opportunities for professional growth and development

Company paid PhilHealth or HMO

13th month

Paid time off



***TO APPLY***

Send a LINK to your resume and LinkedIn

Also, send a note about why you are interested in this position.

WARNING: If you put the job description into an LLM and it just reorganizes the words for your note to us we will reject your application.



Put Research Analyst in subject line

Use 3 apply points



I am Adam Probolsky. I will read your application personally.



This position is available immediately.



About Probolsky Research: We are a woman- and Latina-owned market and opinion research firm working for corporate, election, government, and nonprofit clients.



Other information to help you d
 if you what to apply



We do not hire workers. We hire professionals. If you believe that you should be treated as an equal player in an organization, regardless of where you live, this might be the right position to you.



We need people who can manage up, take initiative, and leave shyness at the door. If you can tell  your boss he/she is wrong, this might be the right position for you.

title: Program Assistant
description:
PROGRAM ASSISTANT

Full-Time | Philippines | $800/month | Must work US Time Zone hours



We aren’t looking for a basic virtual assistant. We’re looking for a Client Accountability Operator who protects retention, enforces standards, and ensures no client slips through the cracks.



At Speak Your Way To Cash®, we’ve grown from zero to an Inc. 5000 company — and we’re just getting started. Our mission is to empower experts and entrepreneurs to turn their voices into revenue.



This role is not about “helping.”

It is about protecting revenue, enforcing follow-through, and keeping our 400+ clients moving.



You will be the operational heartbeat behind our Mastermind and Academy programs. If someone misses a payment, you call. If someone misses a call, you follow up. If engagement drops, you flag it. If a client wins, you capture it.



This is a performance role. Not a passive one.





WHAT YOU’LL DO



You will support the Director of Client Success, Mastermind Coach, and Academy Coach.



Daily Responsibilities

• Monitor missed payments and immediately call clients who fall behind.

• Send payment follow-up
s and document all communication in CRM.

• Track revenue submission forms and flag missing reports.

• Monitor engagement dashboards (Green/Yellow/Red).

• Flag 30+ day inactivity.

• Follow clients on social media and track major life or business updates.

• Alert leadership to major milestones, crises, or contract wins.

• Track missed Q&A or hot seat calls and reach out when clients miss both.

• Update CRM notes and automations.

• Track birthdays and personal milestones.



Weekly Responsibilities

• Update retention dashboards.

• Prepare Red/Yellow/Green client reports for Director review.

• Monitor WhatsApp inactivity and missed submissions.

• Follow up on testimonial opportunities (3–5 month mark).

• Support testimonial collection and coordination.

• Audit onboarding completion (forms, activation milestones).

• Track challenge participation and engagement levels.



Monthly Responsibilities

• Assist in renewal pipeline tracking.

• Compile client wins for internal Wins Report.

• Monitor onboarding completion rates.

• Prepare milestone tracking summaries.

• Support white-glove gift coordination (birthdays, bereavement, celebrations).





CRITICAL FUNCTION: PAYMENT & ACCOUNTABILITY CALLS



If a client misses payment:

• You call within 24 hours.

• You speak clearly, confidently, and professionally.

• You follow a structured script while remaining human.

• You document the outcome immediately.



You must have a strong, confident, polished phone voice. This is mandatory.





WHO THIS ROLE IS FOR



- You are confident on the phone and not afraid of difficult conversations.

- You speak clear, professional English with strong verbal communication skills.

- You have experience in client success, collections, or account management.

- You are organized and love dashboards, trackers, and systems.

- You follow up until something is resolved.

- You understand urgency and move fast.

- You are comfortable enforcing standards politely but firmly.

- You can work US time zone hours consistently.





WHO THIS ROLE ISN’T FOR



- You avoid confrontation.

- You dislike making phone calls.

- You need constant supervision.

- You struggle with follow-through.

- You get uncomfortable asking clients about payments.

- You are looking for a low-pressure job.



This role protects revenue. It requires backbone.





WHAT YOU BRING



• Proven experience in virtual assistance, client support, collections, or CRM management.

• Demonstrated track record of managing multiple clients or accounts simultaneously.

• Experience using CRMs and tracking systems.

• Strong written and spoken English.

• Bachelor’s degree preferred.

• Strategic + tactical thinking with clean execution.

• Strong phone presence with professional tone and confidence.





COMPENSATION & BENEFITS



• $800/month (Full-time).

• Performance-based bonuses possible based on retention and payment recovery.

• Direct impact at a high-growth Inc. 5000 company.

• Visibility, ownership, and growth opportunities into higher client success roles.





HOW TO APPLY



1. Submit this application: 



2.
 me using the information below



: 

Subject line: Program Assistant – Your Name



Include the following:

1. A written sample
 to a client who is 14 days behind on payment.

2. A Loom video (3–5 minutes) explaining:

• Why you want this role

• Why you are confident making payment collection calls

• Why you are a strong fit for a high-performance environment



Applications without both requirements will not be reviewed.





This is not a content company.

This is a results company.



We show up on our clients’ worst day and their biggest contract day.



Apply now. Show us how you’ve done this before, what you’ve built, and how you’ll take this role to the next level.

title: Bid Solicitor
description:
Responsibilities



Market & bench building

*Build and continuously expand TriArc’s qualified trade partner database by trade, geography, capacity, and performance.

*Run ongoing prequalification (financials, safety, workload, bonding) in coordination with Accounting.



Bid strategy & scheduling

*Publish the rolling Bid Calendar; align milestones with Estimators/PMs and owners/architects.

*Break down drawings/specs into clear trade packages; assign scope sheets and alternates.



ITB execution

*Prepare Invitation to Bid packages (drawings, specs, addenda, bid forms, alternates, due dates, delivery method).

*Post to BuildingConnected; segment targeted lists; personalize top-tier outreach.



Relentless follow-up

*3-touch cadence (Day 1
 + Day 3 call/text + Day 5 confirmation) and pre-deadline “nudge” to secure commitments.

*Track interest/no-bid reasons; backfill gaps early; escalate risk areas to Estimating.



Bid intake & leveling

*Receive bids, confirm inclusions/exclusions, and level against scope sheets; flag outliers and VE options.

*Package a clean Bid Leveling Matrix for Estimators/PMs with award recommendation.



Data & feedback loops

*Score and rate trade partner performance post-award (price accuracy, quality, safety, schedule).

*Run post-bid debriefs; capture lessons learned to refine future ITBs.



Brand & relationship

*Be TriArc’s front door for subs: fast answers, fair process, respectful communication, and values-based partnership.





Required Traits & Skills

*Excellent Communication Skills

*Hunter mentality: proactive, persistent, upbeat on the phone; comfortable making 30–50 quality contacts/day in peak cycles.

*Construction fluency: understands typical scopes (CSI divisions), alternates, addenda, and gaps that derail bids.

*Process discipline: lives in checklists, documents everything.

*Communication: crisp writing, professional phone presence, tact with hard-bid deadlines.

*Values fit: Do what you say • Do the right thing • Gumption • Stick-to-itiveness • Passion/Enthusiasm.



Nice-to-Have

*Customer Service / Appointment Setting experience

*GC pre-con/bid desk experience or supplier/manufacturer rep background.

*Existing DFW sub network across interiors, ground-up flex/industrial, retail shells.



Key KPIs & Scorecard (reported weekly)

*% trade packages with 3+ bids (primary KPI)

*Avg. bids per trade

*% bids on time

*coverage gaps prevented (and when caught)

*Prequal completion rate / expirations cured

*Diversity/bench depth by top 15 trades

*ITB Bid cycle time

*Trade partner satisfaction pulse



Sample Weekly Cadence

*Mon: Publish bid calendar; align with Estimators; identify coverage risks.

*Tue–Thu: ITB sends, follow-ups, phone blocks, addendum broadcasts, pre-deadline confirmations.

*Fri: Deliver leveled bid matrices; update KPIs; partner thank-you touchpoints.



Technical Requirements:

*Computer: Intel i5 processor or equivalent with at least 8GB RAM

*Internet: Minimum 50 MBPS download speed

*Dual Monitor is required

*Noise cancellation headset

*HD Cam (Clutter Free, Ideally Plain Well-lit background)



Job Details:

*Job Type: Full-time (40 hours per week)

*Schedule: Monday to Friday 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM CDT - Monday to Friday 9:30 PM - 5:30 AM PHT

*Salary Range: PHP 40,000 – 65,000 monthly, depending on experience.



Application Instruction:

Fill out this form: 
/appqNAvkEQ10YFlc3/pagO7KL90nGSfhiE9/form

After submitting the form, click the apply button and send us a short message on why you would be a good fit for the role.

title: Construction VA
description:
Responsibilities



Market & bench building

*Build and continuously expand TriArc’s qualified trade partner database by trade, geography, capacity, and performance.

*Run ongoing prequalification (financials, safety, workload, bonding) in coordination with Accounting.



Bid strategy & scheduling

*Publish the rolling Bid Calendar; align milestones with Estimators/PMs and owners/architects.

*Break down drawings/specs into clear trade packages; assign scope sheets and alternates.



ITB execution

*Prepare Invitation to Bid packages (drawings, specs, addenda, bid forms, alternates, due dates, delivery method).

*Post to BuildingConnected; segment targeted lists; personalize top-tier outreach.



Relentless follow-up

*3-touch cadence (Day 1
 + Day 3 call/text + Day 5 confirmation) and pre-deadline “nudge” to secure commitments.

*Track interest/no-bid reasons; backfill gaps early; escalate risk areas to Estimating.



Bid intake & leveling

*Receive bids, confirm inclusions/exclusions, and level against scope sheets; flag outliers and VE options.

*Package a clean Bid Leveling Matrix for Estimators/PMs with award recommendation.



Data & feedback loops

*Score and rate trade partner performance post-award (price accuracy, quality, safety, schedule).

*Run post-bid debriefs; capture lessons learned to refine future ITBs.



Brand & relationship

*Be TriArc’s front door for subs: fast answers, fair process, respectful communication, and values-based partnership.





Required Traits & Skills

*Excellent Communication Skills

*Hunter mentality: proactive, persistent, upbeat on the phone; comfortable making 30–50 quality contacts/day in peak cycles.

*Construction fluency: understands typical scopes (CSI divisions), alternates, addenda, and gaps that derail bids.

*Process discipline: lives in checklists, documents everything.

*Communication: crisp writing, professional phone presence, tact with hard-bid deadlines.

*Values fit: Do what you say • Do the right thing • Gumption • Stick-to-itiveness • Passion/Enthusiasm.



Nice-to-Have

*Customer Service / Appointment Setting experience

*GC pre-con/bid desk experience or supplier/manufacturer rep background.

*Existing DFW sub network across interiors, ground-up flex/industrial, retail shells.



Key KPIs & Scorecard (reported weekly)

*% trade packages with 3+ bids (primary KPI)

*Avg. bids per trade

*% bids on time

*coverage gaps prevented (and when caught)

*Prequal completion rate / expirations cured

*Diversity/bench depth by top 15 trades

*ITB Bid cycle time

*Trade partner satisfaction pulse



Sample Weekly Cadence

*Mon: Publish bid calendar; align with Estimators; identify coverage risks.

*Tue–Thu: ITB sends, follow-ups, phone blocks, addendum broadcasts, pre-deadline confirmations.

*Fri: Deliver leveled bid matrices; update KPIs; partner thank-you touchpoints.



Technical Requirements:

*Computer: Intel i5 processor or equivalent with at least 8GB RAM

*Internet: Minimum 50 MBPS download speed

*Dual Monitor is required

*Noise cancellation headset

*HD Cam (Clutter Free, Ideally Plain Well-lit background)



Job Details:

*Job Type: Full-time (40 hours per week)

*Schedule: Monday to Friday 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM CDT - Monday to Friday 9:30 PM - 5:30 AM PHT

*Salary Range: PHP 40,000 – 65,000 monthly, depending on experience.

CapCut Video Editor
description:
JOB RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO:

Short-Form Video Editing (Primary)

*Edit 5–20+ short-form videos per day optimized for TikTok and TikTok Shop

*Edit short video/audio podcast (2 episodes a month)

*Create tight, fast-paced edits where cuts feel natural and almost unnoticeable

*Incorporate:

**Multiple camera angles

**Product close-ups

**Usage demonstrations

**On-screen text and captions that improve retention and conversions

*Enhance videos to match a more premium, brand-friendly aesthetic (lighting, color, polish)

*Adapt edits to current TikTok creative trends and algorithm preferences

*Utilize preferred settings in CapCut and suggest others that would make content better



Creative Input & Optimization

*Provide creative suggestions such as:

**What shots are missing

**When pacing needs to be faster

**When structure should change to improve hook and retention

*Help improve content quality and viewer retention over time, not just execute instructions



Fast Turnaround & Workflow

*Deliver edits on very tight timelines, often within 24 hours

*Organize files, exports, and versions efficiently

*Work within a fast-changing content schedule that may shift daily



AI-Assisted Editing & Localization (Future Phase)

*Learn and use AI tools to:

**Speed up editing workflows

**Assist with repurposing content

**Support future Spanish-language versions using AI voice and lip-sync tools (training provided)

*Follow new AI-based workflows as systems are implemented



Cross-Platform Repurposing (Secondary)

*Prepare videos for reposting on:

**Instagram Reels

*
 Reels

*Maintain correct formats, captions, and technical specs per platform



EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS REQUIRED:

*3+ years of video production experience

*Experience with remote team tools like Time Doctor, Slack, Trello, Google Suite, LastPass,
 and Zoom

*Excellent English skills, and able to communicate well both verbally and in writing

*Proven expertise in editing short-form TikTok content, preferably using CapCut (not YouTube-style long-form videos)

*Strong understanding of:

**TikTok pacing and hooks

**Product-driven storytelling

**TikTok Shop style content

*Ability to edit:

**Talking-head videos

**Product demos

**Lifestyle shots

**Voiceover videos

*Strong eye for:

**Timing

**Visual flow

**Brand presentation

*Content that converts sales and retains viewers

*Comfortable working in a high-volume, fast-paced environment

*Open to learning and using AI tools to improve efficiency

*Excellent organization and file management



TECH REQUIREMENTS:

*Computer - at least i7 or equivalent with 16gb RAM

*Internet Speed - At least 100 MBPS Download speed



Job Type: Part-Time (potentially move into full-time depending on business needs and performance ) - 4 hours daily / 20 hours weekly

Schedule: 3PM-7PM EST M-F // 4AM-8AM PHT

Salary Range: PHP 30,000- PHP 40,000 monthly, Depending on experience

title: Development & Digital Marketing Specialist
description:
Development and Digital Marketing Specialist



Work Hours: Mon-Fri, 20-30 hours per week. Shift may be required to start as early as 4AM PH Time



About the Firm:

Our immigration law firm specializes in providing expert legal services to individuals, families, and businesses navigating the complexities of U.S. immigration law. We are a dedicated team of professionals committed to delivering top-quality support and guidance to our clients.



Position Overview:

The Business Development & Digital Marketing Specialist is responsible for expanding the company’s online presence and driving business growth through digital marketing, networking, lead generation, and sales initiatives. This role focuses on building relationships with potential clients and business partners, managing online platforms and social media, creating advertising campaigns, and converting leads into clients.



Other Skills / Requirements:

•Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Communications, or a related field (or equivalent work experience).

•Proven experience in business development, sales, digital marketing, or lead generation.

•Strong understanding of online marketing platforms, social media management, and digital advertising (e.g.,
, LinkedIn, Google Ads, or similar platforms).

•Experience in B2B and B2P networking, client outreach, and relationship building.

•Demonstrated ability to generate, track, and convert leads into clients or business opportunities.

•Excellent communication skills.

•Ability to manage multiple campaigns, leads, and priorities in a fast-paced environment.

•Experience working with professional services, legal, immigration, or consulting firms is an advantage but not required.

•Must be knowledgeable in google SEO and with sales experience.



Duties:

•Prepare, implement, and oversee online, B2B, and B2P networking strategies to generate new business opportunities.

•Create, manage, and optimize digital advertisements across online platforms, websites, and social media channels.

•Manage and maintain the company’s social media accounts, ensuring consistent branding, engagement, and content posting.

•Develop and execute lead generation strategies to attract potential clients and partner businesses with the help of the marketing and sales team.

•Oversee the tracking, monitoring, and reporting of leads, conversions, and campaign performance using appropriate tools and reports.

•Reach out to, engage, and build relationships with prospective clients, partners, and businesses through
, social platforms, and other outreach channels.

•Coordinate with internal teams to ensure accurate messaging, branding, and alignment with company goals.

•Oversee the maintenance updated database of leads, contacts, and business opportunities.

•Identify new market opportunities, platforms, and trends to support business growth.

•Perform other sales, marketing, and business development tasks as assigned.



Department: Marketing

Note: This is an independent contractor position.



Only those applicants who submit their applications on this link will be screened:


/form/

title: Part-Time Real Estate Operations & Executive Assistant
description:
Position: Part-Time Real Estate Operations & Executive Assistant (20 hrs/week)

Rate: $5-6/hour

Schedule: Part-time with potential to grow into full-time



About the Role

I’m looking for a highly dependable assistant to work with me directly — 1:1 — inside a fast-moving curative-title real estate business. You will NOT be doing heir research. Your job is to keep operations smooth, organized, and moving forward. That means handling closing prep, transaction coordination, follow-ups, admin work, and stepping in when problems pop up.



This role is for someone who takes ownership, thinks ahead, and can support the team without being babysat.



Core Responsibilities

• Prepare closing packages and assist with dispositions + TC

• Follow up with sellers, buyers, heirs, and title contacts already in process

• Keep CRM and files updated, organized, and maintained

• Handle administrative support: scheduling, docs, communication, file management

• Solve operational issues (tech, CRM, admin, HR-type task requests) as they arise

• Act as my personal executive assistant — working closely with me day-to-day

• Keep 
ntum going when others need direction or clarity



Requirements

• Strong experience in real estate admin, TC, escrow/closing, or operations

• Excellent written and spoken English with a neutral/very light accent

• Highly organized with strong attention to detail

• Problem solver who takes initiative without waiting for step-by-step instructions

• Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace

• Must be committed — no juggling multiple jobs

• Long-term mindset with the goal of growing to full-time



To Apply (Mandatory)

Send ALL of the following:

- Your resume

- A short message explaining your real estate/TC/admin experience

- A Loom video (1–2 minutes) introducing yourself and explaining why you’re a high performer



Applicants who do not include a Loom video will not be considered.

title: Amazon FBM Product Sourcing Specialist
description:
Amazon FBM Sourcing Specialist Full-time · Remote · Philippines-based



---



About the role



We are a growing Amazon FBM (Fulfilled by Merchant) seller looking for a detail-oriented, self-sufficient, and highly motivated product sourcing specialist to help us identify and secure profitable products. You'll be a core part of our sourcing operation — researching competitor storefronts, building distributor relationships, and mining wholesale catalogs to find our next winning products. We expect our tea
mbers to be proactive, results-driven, and committed to growing the business.



---



What you'll do



- Perform reverse storefront sourcing by identifying competitors' current profitable products on Amazon and reverse-engineering the supply chain behind them

- Research and identify relevant wholesale distributors and brands that supply those products

- Sign up for distributor and wholesale accounts on behalf of the company

- Follow up consistently with distributors to obtain product catalogs, pricelists, and account approvals

- Parse and analyze wholesale distributor price lists and catalogs to identify products with strong profit potential

- Run profitability checks on sourced products using Seller Amp (factoring in Amazon fees, shipping costs, and sell-through rates) and log results clearly in Google Sheets

- Maintain a well-organized sourcing tracker / master sheet in Google Sheets documenting all leads, distributor contacts, product viability notes, and follow-up statuses

- Continuously expand our sourcing pipeline beyond reverse sourcing — using distributor catalogs as an ongoing source of new product opportunities

- Flag high-potential products with clear profitability notes for team review



---



What we're looking for



- Hands-on experience with Seller Amp, Keepa, and Google Sheets — these are required, not optional

- Demonstrated experience with reverse storefront sourcing for Amazon - you should be able to do this from day one without training

- Experience with wholesale sourcing: finding distributors, applying for accounts, and following up to obtain catalogs

- Ability to interpret Keepa data (rank history, price history, sales velocity) to assess product viability

- Strong attention to detail when logging product data and profitability metrics

- Self-starter who takes ownership and doesn't need to be micromanaged

- Comfortable working through large distributor catalog files (CSV/Excel/Google Sheets)

- Solid written English for distributor outreach and communications

- Reliable internet connection with availability during US business hours (or significant overlap)



---



Good to have



- Experience with Amazon FBM specifically (not just FBA)

- Previous experience building or maintaining sourcing SOPs or master sheets

- Background supporting a US-based Amazon seller

- Hands-on experience successfully ungating brands or categories on Amazon



---



Key deliverables



- Regularly updated sourcing tracker with all leads, viability assessments, and distributor pipeline status

- Consistent weekly output of new product leads reviewed and logged (quota to be agreed upon during onboarding)

- Active distributor pipeline: accounts applied for, followed up on, and catalogs received and parsed

- Clean, well-documented profitability notes for every product evaluated



---



Job details



Job type: Full-time, long-term

Location: Remote (Philippines)

Hours: 40 hrs/week

Experience level: 1+ years



---



Salary



$480 - $650 per month USD depending on experience and performance. After your first 4 paid weeks, we conduct a salary review based on your performance and results.



We do offer occasional bonuses for truly exceptional results, such as sourcing a high-value distributor or an unusually profitable product opportunity. These are not guaranteed but are given at our discretion when results go above and beyond.



---



Confidentiality requirement



All product leads, sourcing data, distributor contacts, and any information uncovered during your work are strictly confidential and the exclusive property of our business. You are not permitted to share, use, or disclose any leads or sourcing information with any third party, other sellers, or for personal use. This is a non-negotiable condition of employment.



---



How to apply





To apply, send a short message answering the following questions:



- What is your experience with Amazon sourcing — FBM or FBA?

- Have you done reverse storefront sourcing before? If yes, describe how you do it.

- Have you signed up with wholesale distributors and parsed catalogs? Give an example.

- Which tools have you personally used — Seller Amp, Keepa, Google Sheets?

- Share one specific product or distributor you have successfully sourced in the past.



Applications that do not answer all five questions will not be considered.

title: Procurement Support Assistant
description:
About the Company

IAT Solutions LLC is a U.S.-based company that sources and supplies industrial and military-grade parts. We work with manufacturers and authorized distributors to find competitive pricing and fulfill orders for our clients. We are growing and looking for reliable tea
mbers to help us scale our operations.



Role Overview

We are looking for a detail-oriented and proactive Virtual Assistant to handle vendor outreach and quote sourcing for our bidding operations. Your primary job is simple: contact vendors by
, online quote forms, and phone to get pricing on parts we need for government bids.



You will be given a list of parts that need quotes. For each part, you will reach out to the manufacturer or authorized distributor, request a quote, and report the pricing back to the company. You are expected to return a minimum of 50 quotes per day. Speed, persistence, and accuracy are essential.



Full training will be provided on our internal systems and processes.



Key Responsibilities

• Receive daily lists of parts that need vendor pricing

• Research and identify the correct manufacturer or authorized distributor for each part

• Contact vendors via
 to request formal quotes, including part numbers, quantities, and delivery requirements

• Submit quote requests through vendor websites and online quote/inquiry forms

• Call vendors by phone to request quotes, follow up on pending requests, and confirm pricing details

• Record all received quotes accurately in our internal system, including unit price, lead time, and any special notes

• Follow up with vendors who have not responded to ensure quotes are received before bid deadlines

• Send quote summaries to the company owner for review and bid decisions

• Maintain organized records of vendor contacts and communications

• Meet a daily minimum of 50 quotes returned to the company



How You Will Contact Vendors

•
 — Send structured quote requests to vendor sales teams with all required part and delivery details

• Online Quote Forms — Submit requests through manufacturer and distributor websites when direct
 contacts are not available

• Phone Calls — Call vendor sales lines to request quotes, follow up on outstanding requests, and verify pricing. Professional and confident phone communication is required.



Required Skills & Qualifications

• Experience in sourcing, procurement, purchasing, or vendor outreach

• Excellent written and spoken English — you will communicate with U.S.-based vendors daily

• Comfortable making outbound phone calls to vendors and suppliers

• Strong attention to detail when working with part numbers, pricing, and specifications

• Proficient with web-based platforms, spreadsheets, and
 (Gmail or Outlook)

• Reliable internet connection and a quiet workspace for phone calls

• Self-motivated and able to work independently with minimal supervision

• Able to consistently meet daily output targets

• Available to work during US Eastern business hours, Monday through Friday



Preferred (Nice to Have)

• Experience sourcing industrial, automotive, or heavy equipment parts

• Familiarity with military or industrial parts procurement

• Experience using CRM or order management platforms

title: Lettings Admin & Progression Coordinator (UK Property Experience Required)
description:

Lettings process (enquiry ? move-in)

UK compliance basics

Strong written English

You require constant instructions

Smooth tenancy progression

Inbox always organized

Use “Vixa Hire” as your subject line.

Cover Letter

Internet Speed Test Result

Screenshot of your Laptop Specs

title: Recruiter
description:
I am looking for a part-time Recruiter. This role requires someone who has solid talent acquisition experience and can manage end-to-end recruitment for a wide range of roles across different industries. You’ll partner directly with leadership and collaborate closely with fellow recruiters and the client success team to deliver high-quality candidates and a smooth hiring process.



Responsibilities:

- Manage full-cycle recruitment: sourcing, screening, interviewing, assessments, client endorsement, and job offer coordination.

- Recruit for a wide range of positions across various industries and functional areas.

- Communicate with clients to understand role requirements, clarify expectations, and provide recruitment updates.

- Coordinate candidate interviews, availability, and follow-ups.

- Build and maintain talent pipelines for recurring and hard-to-fill roles.

- Use multiple sourcing channels, job boards, and Boolean search strategies to attract qualified candidates.

- Prepare candidate profiles and other necessary files for endorsement.

- Work closely with the client success team to ensure strong employer–candidate alignment.

- Maintain accurate documentation, candidate records, trackers, and reporting tools.

- Contribute ideas to improve internal recruitment processes, systems, and hiring strategies.



Qualifications:

- Minimum 3 years of experience in end-to-end recruitment, preferably within a virtual staffing or BPO/RPO environment.

- Proven end-to-end recruitment experience, handling multiple roles across different industries.

- Strong client communication and client management skills, preferably foreign clients.

- Experienced in candidate coordination and managing high-volume pipelines.

- Solid background in sourcing through job portals, social media, databases, referrals, and Boolean search techniques.

- Tech-savvy and able to use ATS tools, spreadsheets, and communication platforms efficiently.

- Comfortable working independently while collaborating with a distributed team.

- Strong attention to detail, time management, and ability to meet deadlines.

- Ability to work part-time within US business hours (Pacific/Mountain time), with the possibility of going full-time depending on demand and work performance.



Apply here:

title: Finance & Office Manager
description:
About the Role

We are a fast-growing Australian-based accounting and financial services group seeking a capable Finance & Office Manager to manage internal finance and office operations across our group of businesses.



Our group operates across multiple divisions including bookkeeping, tax, finance broking, real estate, and global talent services. As the business continues to grow, we are looking for someone who can take ownership of iinternal operational matters, ensuring that the firm runs efficiently behind the scenes.



This role will manage the firm’s internal finance, office administration, compliance coordination, and practice management processes, allowing the leadership team to focus on client service and business growth.



The ideal candidate will have strong accounting capability, excellent organisational skills, and the ability to manage multiple internal functions simultaneously.



This position reports to the Operations Manager and works closely with internal teams to ensure smooth daily operations across the organisation. This role is expected to take ownership of all internal operational matters of the firm, including internal finance, office administration, compliance coordination, telecommunications management, marketing coordination, and practice management.



The Finance & Office Manager will work closely with the Operations Manager to ensure internal systems run efficiently and that the organisation operates in a structured and well-managed manner.



As the business continues to grow, the successful candidate will have the opportunity to build and manage internal processes and support functions, helping strengthen the operational backbone of the group.



Key Responsibilities

1. Internal Finance Management

Oversee internal financial administration across group entities, including:

• Bank reconciliations across group companies

• Accounts Payable and Accounts Receivable management

• Internal billing administration and monitoring

• Expense tracking and cost monitoring

• Payroll administration support and superannuation coordination

• Supporting end-of-month financial administration including revenue tracking, gross profit monitoring and expense allocation

• Inter-company cross charges and internal allocations

• Supporting internal financial reporting preparation

• Supporting quarterly compliance preparation (BAS, IAS and related reporting)



2. Office Management

Manage internal office operations and administrative systems to ensure smooth day-to-day business operations.

Responsibilities include:

• Office administration and operational coordination

• Telecommunications management (phone systems, internet services and providers)

• Office facilities and vendor coordination

• Office systems administration and operational procedures

• Supporting internal office team coordination

• Monitoring administrative workflow efficiency

• Maintaining organised operational documentation and records



3. Marketing & Internal Coordination

Support internal marketing and communication coordination.

This includes:

• Coordinating marketing administration and campaign activities

• Supporting internal marketing initiatives

• Maintaining marketing materials and documentation

• Coordinating with external marketing partners when required

• Supporting internal communication initiatives



4. Compliance & Practice Management

Support internal governance and operational compliance across the firm.

Responsibilities include:

• Maintaining internal compliance calendars

• Coordinating annual administration including insurance renewals, workers compensation administration and internal compliance tracking

• Supporting practice management systems and processes

• Ensuring internal documentation and procedures remain organised and up to date



Reporting Structure

This role reports to the Operations Manager and works closely with internal teams to manage the firm’s internal finance and office operations.

The Finance & Office Manager will play an important role in ensuring all internal matters of the firm are properly managed and organised, supporting the overall efficiency of the business.



Requirements

• Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance

• Minimum 2 years accounting or bookkeeping experience

• Strong understanding of bookkeeping and financial administration

• Excellent organisational and coordination skills

• Strong attention to detail and accountability

• Ability to manage multiple internal operational responsibilities

• Proficiency with accounting software and Microsoft 365 / Google Workspace

• Strong written and verbal communication skills

• Demonstrated ability to take ownership of internal processes and improve operational systems will be highly regarded



Why Join Us

Our group is a growing multi-disciplinary business with strong 
ntum.



This role offers:

• Exposure to internal finance and operational management

• Opportunity to develop into a senior internal management role as the business expands

• Hands-on involvement across multiple businesses and industries

• Long-term career opportunities within an expanding organisation

title: Video Editor — Supplement Ad Creatives (AI-Assisted, Meta Ads)
description:
We run supplement brands selling direct-to-consumer through Meta 
/Instagram) ads. Our business is built on high-converting video ad creatives — and we need a skilled video editor to become a core part of our creative team.



**One thing to know upfront:** In our world, the hook makes or breaks the ad. The first 1–3 seconds determine whether someone stops scrolling or keeps going. If you don't obsess over hooks, this isn't the right role.



This is not a one-off project. We're looking for someone who wants to grow with us long-term, take ownership of the creative output, and eventually become our go-to person for all things video.



**The Challenge**



We have little to no actual product footage. That means you'll need to be resourceful — using AI generation tools, stock footage, UGC-style clips, and creative compositing to build ads that look polished, authentic, and conversion-ready. If you're the type of editor who can make something out of nothing, this role is for you.



**What You'll Be Doing**



- Edit video ads (1–10 minutes) optimized for Meta platforms 
, Instagram, Reels) — specifically for supplement products

- **Create multiple hook variations for each ad concept** — this is a massive part of the job. Different openings, different visual angles, different pain points. We test hooks aggressively because the hook is the single most important element of any ad we run

- Generate AI B-roll of the product, lifestyle scenes, and talking-head content using tools like HeyGen, Higgsfield, Kling, Runway, Pika, and similar platforms

- Source and curate stock footage, UGC clips, testimonial-style content, and health/wellness B-roll to build compelling supplement ad creatives

- Add text overlays, captions, transitions, sound design, and music to maximize engagement and watch time

- Adapt and localize winning international supplement ad creatives for our target market

- Work from scripts and briefs we provide — but also bring your own creative eye and suggestions

- Deliver fast turnarounds with clean, organized project files



**Requirements**



*Must Have*



- Proficient in Adobe Premiere Pro or CapCut (or both)

- Hands-on experience with AI video generation tools — HeyGen, Higgsfield, Kling, Runway, Pika, or similar. You should already be using these, not just aware of them

- Ability to create professional-looking product and lifestyle visuals with minimal or zero real footage

- **A deep understanding of what makes a strong hook** — you know that the first few seconds are everything, and you have ideas for how to stop the scroll, not just edit what comes after it

- Strong understanding of video ad pacing, retention techniques, and storytelling structure

- Experience editing direct-response or e-commerce video ads (not just vlogs or YouTube content)

- Ability to source and work with stock footage, UGC content, and product clips

- Good eye for typography, color grading, and visual hierarchy in ad creatives

- Reliable internet connection and able to work during overlapping business hours (GST/Gulf time preferred)

- Strong communication skills — you ask questions when the brief is unclear, not after delivery



*Nice to Have*



- Experience editing ads specifically for supplements, health, or wellness products

- Experience with After Effects for motion graphics and animated text

- Familiarity with what makes ads perform on Meta (thumb-stop rate, hold rate, CTR)

- Experience working with product-based businesses or dropshipping/e-commerce brands

- Ability to create simple product mockups or lifestyle composites

- Understanding of supplement ad compliance (health claims, before/after restrictions, etc.)



**Why Work With Us**



- Stable, long-term position — not a one-time gig

- Clear feedback loop: you deliver ? we give feedback ? you improve ? repeat until we're fully aligned

- Room to grow into a lead creative role as we scale

- Performance bonuses for ads that scale well (we reward results, not just hours)

- We respect your craft — you may know more than us about certain things and we're happy to learn from you too

- We pay properly and on time, every time



**How to Apply — Paid Test Task**



**Important:** Do NOT just send your resume. We use a short paid test task to find the right person. Here's how it works:



1. We'll send you a supplement product and a script for a 2–6 minute video ad

2. You'll create 1–2 edited versions of the video, **each with 3 different hooks (openings)** — because again, the hook is everything. We want to see how you think about those critical first few seconds

3. We provide the script only — you source the footage, generate AI B-roll of the product, choose the music, handle the pacing and editing

4. We'll evaluate: **hook quality first**, then edit quality, pacing, AI footage quality, content sourcing, creativity, turnaround time, and how well you follow the brief

5. You'll be paid for the test task regardless of the outcome



This tells us more than any portfolio ever could. We want to see how you work, how you think, and how fast you move — not just your highlight reel.



**To Apply, Please Include:**



- A short introduction about yourself and your experience with video editing for ads

- Links to 2–3 examples of video ads you've edited (supplements or health products preferred, but any direct-response work is fine)

- Examples of AI-generated video content you've created (even short clips count — we want to see you're actually using these tools)

- Your editing software and AI tools of choice

- Your availability (hours per day / timezone)

- Confirm that you're ready to complete the paid test task



*We're not looking for perfection on day one. We're looking for someone who cares about the craft, takes feedback well, and wants to get better every single week. If that's you, apply now.*

title: Meta ads Video Editor
description:
Do you want to be a part of our video team growth journey? Send us a message!



About Our 5+ Year Old Company

We are not like most companies. We focus heavily on video advertisement, and that’s why 30% out of the 20 employees in the company work solely with video. In this in-house position, you’ll be a part of a video editing team that is obsessed with telling stories that drive real results. As the competition in the e-commerce beauty industry is hard, our videos must stand out.



Role Overview

As a part of our video editing team, you’ll work closely with the team lead and the other video editors, to make storytelling video advertisements. The videos are mainly for paid ads on Instagram,
 and 
, and you’ll get rapid feedback daily on your progress.



Your Responsibilities

Enhance the storytelling by adding music, sound effects, pacing and transitions

When it boosts the storytelling, utilize AI to improve essential parts of the story

Create multiple attention-grabbing hooks that gets the viewer on the hook

Edit raw material into engaging, storytelling and converting video ads



The Requirements

Proven experience editing e-commerce or direct-response ads

Minimum 3 years of experience with Adobe Premiere Pro and After Effects

Willingness to learn new skills, expertise, tools and AI



As A Person You Are

Creative and optimistic

Reliable and accountable

Detail-oriented & perfectionist



What We Offer The Right Person

A long-term remote position with a stable company (5+ years)

Collaborative environment with experienced video editors

Opportunity to grow with a fast-scaling e-commerce brand



What We Expect In Return

Full-time commitment (40 hours/week, Monday–Friday)

Drive to learn, openness for feedback and team spirit



Did you get excited when you read this? Then send us a message and we’ll get back to you!

, pitching, and follow-ups to secure partnership opportunities.

 management)

title: Executive Assistant to the CEO
description:

Before we review your resume, all applicants are required to submit a short 2–4 minute video.

We are not looking for perfect answers. 



We are looking for how you think, how you operate, and how you communicate.

You will not simply manage calendars or send
s. You will work directly with the CEO as a right hand operator, helping turn ideas into execution and ensuring priorities move forward.

mbers accountable to timelines and outcomes

title: Part-Time Flutter & .NET Developer — Remote
description:
About Us - Lynx Software LLC is a Miami-based digital agency and software development firm building real-world applications for clients across multiple industries. We move fast, write clean code, and don't babysit our developers.



The Role - We're looking for a skilled, self-sufficient developer to join our team part-time on Rekloze — a real estate transaction management application that helps agents and brokers track contracts, deadlines, and critical milestones with automated reminders and notifications.



This is not an entry-level role. You will be expected to hit the ground running.



What You'll Build

Flutter mobile/web UI for real estate transaction workflows

Contract deadline tracking with automated reminder 
 Web API backend powering the application

SQL Server database design, queries, and stored procedures

Clean, maintainable, documented code — every time



Requirements

3+ years hands-on Flutter development (not just exposure — shipped apps)


 Web API experience (REST, authentication, middleware)

SQL Server proficiency — schema design, indexing, stored procedures

Ability to work independently with minimal direction

Fluent written English for async team communication

Available for regular overlap with US Eastern Time



A Note on Code Quality We take pride in what we build. Every developer on our team is expected to write code that is clean, well-structured, and easy for others to read and maintain. This isn't about perfection — it's about professionalism and respect for the codebase and your teammates.



Nice to Have

Experience with notification/reminder systems (push,
, SMS)

Real estate, legal, or contract management app background

Familiarity with Jira for task management



What We Offer

$7–$10/hr based on demonstrated experience

Part-time, fully remote — work from anywhere

Long-term engagement for the right person

Direct access to decision-makers — no corporate bureaucracy



To Apply - Send the following to 

Your resume or LinkedIn

2–3 examples of Flutter apps you've built (links or APKs)

A brief description of a .NET + SQL Server project you've worked on

Your available hours per week and timezone

Share a GitHub repo or code sample you're proud of — we review code quality as part of our evaluation process.

Applications without portfolio samples will not be reviewed.

title: Outbound Lead Generation Specialist
description:
**Outbound Lead Generation Specialist (Base Pay + Commission + Performance-Based Role)**



We’re building out the outbound engine for Rope VAI — and we’re looking for someone who wants to **own it like a business, not treat it like a job**.



This is not a clock-in, clock-out role. This is for someone competitive, driven, and hungry to build something real.



---



???? About the Opportunity



Rope VAI helps service-based businesses **capture and convert inbound calls using AI voice systems** — replacing missed calls, slow follow-ups, and manual processes with real-time, human-sounding automation.



This is a **high-demand, high-retention service**:



* Businesses are actively losing revenue from missed calls

* Once installed, the system becomes **mission-critical**

* Clients stay because it directly impacts their revenue



???? You’re not selling hype — you’re putting something in front of the market that businesses *need*



---



???? Your Role



You’ll be responsible for building and scaling our outbound pipeline across:



* Cold

* LinkedIn DMs

*
 & Instagram outreach



Your job is simple:

???? **Start conversations ? qualify ? book demos**



You will:



* Execute high-volume outreach daily

* Test and improve messaging/scripts

* Manage leads and follow-ups

* Continuously optimize performance



This is a **performance-driven role with full ownership** over outbound.



---



???? Compensation Structure



**Recurring Commission:**



* $20/month per closed client

* Paid monthly for as long as the client stays active



**Tiered Base Pay (performance-based):**



* 5 sales ? $50/week

* 10 sales ? $100/week

* 20 sales ? $150/week

* 30 sales ? $200/week

* 50 sales ? $250/week



**Bonuses & incentives available for top performers**



???? The goal is to build **long-term recurring income**, not one-off payouts



---



### ???? Who This Is For



We’re looking for someone who:



* Treats this like **running their own division**

* Is fast, competitive, and self-driven

* Has strong English communication skills

* Is organized and detail-oriented

* Understands sales, scripts, and volume

* Has experience with cold outreach 
 + DMs)

* Doesn’t take rejection personally

* Is constantly looking to improve and win



---



?? Who This Is NOT For



* People who need constant supervision

* People looking for guaranteed salary with low output

* People who take rejection personally



---



???? Why This Role is Different



* You’re building a **recurring income stream**, not chasing one-time commissions

* You’re working with a product that **directly increases revenue for clients**

* You have **ownership over a core growth channel**

* High upside for the right person who executes



---



If you’re someone who wants to **take ownership, move fast, and build something meaningful**, this is for you.



Apply to my
 with the following: 



* Your experience in cold outreach

* Results you’ve generated

* Why you’re a fit for this role

title: Elite Executive Assistant For CEO
description:
>>Elite Executive Assistant to CEO For Online Businesses



Full-Time | Remote | Working U.S. Central Hours | Work Directly with the Founder



We’re hiring an Executive Assistant to Austin Netzley, CEO of 2X.



This is not a typical assistant role.



This is for someone who wants to operate as a true right hand to a founder — helping build, organize, and execute across both business and life.



If you love systems, execution, speed, and turning chaos into clarity, this role will be incredibly rewarding.



>> About 2X & 

At 2X (2X.co), we help founders scale their businesses while getting their time back. We have coaching programs, online community, and in-person events. 



At 
, we help small businesses implement the proven systems and strategies to optimize their team and operations so they can scale faster with more freedom. 



We focus on building businesses that:

- Run on systems (not the founder)

- Scale with more profit and less complexity

- Create real freedom and long-term value



We move fast, operate at a high standard, and care deeply about doing meaningful work that impacts entrepreneurs and their families. 



>> The Mission of This Role



Your mission is simple:



Protect and free up the CEO’s time as a right hand partner to help handle a lot of the details and tasks that can bog him down. 



You will operate as a leverage multiplier — ensuring the right things get done, at the right time, with high quality.



This role is about ownership, anticipation, and execution… with a strong attention to detail, a love for systems, producing efficiently, and being able to wear numerous hats with different tasks and responsibilities. 



>> The Outcomes You Own

This role is responsible for ensuring:



1. CEO Time is Protected and Optimized

Calendar reflects true priorities, not noise.



2. Communication is Clean and Controlled

, Slack, and external communication are organized, timely, and proactive.



3. Projects Move Without Bottlenecks

Key initiatives progress quickly without the CEO needing to follow up.



4. Personal + Business Logistics Run Seamlessly

Travel, scheduling, events, and life logistics are handled smoothly.



5. Events Are Executed at a High Level

Masterminds and experiences are well-organized and on-brand.



6. The Team Stays Aligned and Supported

Basic HR, onboarding, and internal coordination run cleanly.



7. Systems Are Built (Not Just Tasks Completed)

Work becomes organized, repeatable, and scalable over time.



>> Core Responsibilities



1. Executive Support (Business)

- Own
 + communication flow

- Manage calendar with strong prioritization

- Coordinate business travel and logistics

- Conduct research and prep materials

- Support slides, docs, and key initiatives

- Maintain ClickUp task organization



2. Personal Support

- Plan and coordinate personal travel

- Schedule personal appointments and events

- Manage gifts and special occasions

- Handle health, insurance, and admin logistics

- Coordinate with household support



3. Event Coordination (Masterminds)

- Coordinate venues, food, and activities

- Manage communication with clients, vendors, and speakers

- Support on-site execution (when applicable)

- Maintain event systems and checklists



4. Team & HR Support

- Support team communication and culture

- Track reports, scorecards, and metrics

- Coordinate onboarding and access for new hires

- Manage team gifts, time off, and logistics

- Assist with hiring and screening when needed



5. Marketing & Founder Support (Light)

- Coordinate with media team as needed

- Support social posting or content logistics

- Assist with podcast and PR coordination



>> The Ideal Candidate

You are not just organized — you are operationally sharp.



You likely:

- Learn fast and figure things out without hand-holding

- Are extremely organized and detail-oriented

- Execute quickly while maintaining quality

- Anticipate needs before being told

- Take full ownership of outcomes

- Communicate clearly and professionally

- Are tech-savvy and comfortable with tools

- Use AI tools to increase speed and output

- Thrive in fast-paced, high-performance environments



You are the type of person who:

Sees something messy ? fixes it ? systemizes it ? improves it.



>> What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days)

Within your first 60–90 days:

- CEO calendar is always clean, structured, and optimized

- Communication is streamlined and proactive

- Travel and logistics run smoothly without friction

- Key projects are moving forward without bottlenecks

- Event systems are organized and ready for execution

- Core workflows are documented and improving



>> Why This Role Is Different

Direct access to the founder

- You’ll work side-by-side with the CEO on real decisions and execution.

High ownership, not task-taking

- You are expected to think, improve, and operate — not just follow instructions.

Massive learning curve

- You’ll see how a high-performing founder operates across business and life.

Variety + speed

- No two weeks look the same — this role touches everything.

Opportunity to grow



>> Logistics

- Location: Remote position

- Hours: 40 hours/week

- Compensation: based on experience and skills 



We are looking for a long-term fit only. There will be numerous opportunities to advance into other roles if you desire, but this EA role is ideally a long-term position for you.



>> How to Apply

If you’re someone who wants to operate as a true right hand — and take ownership in a fast-moving environment — we’d love to hear from you.



Apply with:

- Your resume

- A short paragraph or few lines on why this role excites you

- Your
 address 



We’ll then review and be in touch!

title: Project Manager
description:
Job Title: Project Manager (Digital Marketing & Coaching Focus)

Remote | Full-Time | Cross-Functional Teams



We’re looking for a highly organized and proactive Project Manager with a passion for digital marketing, personal development, and coaching. In this role, you won’t just manage timelines—you’ll lead client and internal projects from start to finish, ensuring everything runs smoothly, efficiently, and with purpose.



If you thrive in fast-paced environments, enjoy working with creative and strategic teams, and know how to keep digital campaigns on track while fostering positive relationships, this one’s for you.



As our Project Manager, you’ll own multiple client and internal projects across the coaching and digital marketing space. You’ll lead initiatives related to content creation, campaign launches,
 marketing, funnel builds, virtual events, and more—coordinating across departments like design, copywriting, and tech.



You’ll be the central point of alignment, keeping deliverables on track, supporting client needs, and ensuring deadlines are met without sacrificing quality.



Key Responsibilities

-Lead the planning, execution, and delivery of digital marketing & coaching-related projects

-Serve as the primary liaison between internal teams, stakeholders, and clients

-Define project goals, timelines, and deliverables aligned with business and coaching outcomes

-Coordinate across content, design, automation, and client success teams

-Manage timelines using tools like ClickUp, Trello, or Asana

-Run daily/weekly stand-ups, team syncs, and progress check-ins

-Create and improve SOPs, workflows, and systems that enhance team efficiency

-Maintain organized documentation, task tracking, and communication logs



What We're Looking For

-2–5 years of project management experience, preferably in digital marketing or coaching industries

-Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams and juggle multiple active projects

-Familiarity with marketing campaigns, launches, or coaching program operations

-Strong experience using project/task management tools (ClickUp, Asana, Trello, etc.)

-Exceptional communication and leadership skills

-Highly detail-oriented, results-driven, and comfortable in remote work environments



Bonus: Experience with funnels,
 platforms (e.g., HighLevel, ActiveCampaign), and coaching client onboarding



What We Offer

-100% remote work with a collaborative, mission-driven team

-The opportunity to manage high-impact projects in coaching and personal development

-Flexible schedule with a culture of ownership and accountability

-Room for growth into strategic or leadership roles

-A team that values clarity, purpose, and transformation—in work and in life



Apply

title: GoHighLevel Specialist
description:
Remote Work Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

* Work Hours: Availability from 9 AM to 6 PM US CST, Monday–Friday

* Reliable Technology: Stable internet connection (min. 25 Mbps down/up) and a dedicated computer

* Hubstaff Usage: Mandatory time-tracking and productivity monitoring via Hubstaff

* Video Meetings: Cameras must remain on for all video calls

* Full-Time Focus: This is a full-time role; no simultaneous employment with other clients or employers



Buzz Digital Agency is seeking a dedicated GoHighLevel Automation & Integrations Specialist to join our fully remote team in the Philippines. You will architect, deploy, and maintain complex automations that power our clients’ marketing funnels and internal workflows. This role is critical to ensuring seamless data flows, on-time campaign launches, and high deliverability across SMS,
, and voice channels.



If you’re passionate about automation, have a knack for problem-solving, and thrive in a fast-paced digital-marketing environment, we want to hear from you!



Responsibilities

* GoHighLevel Sub-Account Management: End-to-end setup and maintenance of client sub-accounts, including custom domain configuration and SMS/phone A2P registration

* Automation Design & Implementation: Build, test, and deploy automated workflows using n8n, 
, and Zapier to streamline lead capture, follow-up sequences, and data syncing

* API Integration & Logic Flow: Develop and maintain robust integrations via APIs; design clear logic flows and process maps for complex automation scenarios

* Ongoing Monitoring & Optimization: Regularly audit existing automations and integrations for performance, security, and accuracy; implement improvements as needed

* Documentation & Training: Create and update technical documentation; train internal tea
mbers and clients on platform features and best practices

* Troubleshooting & Support: Serve as the escalation point for any automation failures or platform outages; resolve issues swiftly to minimize downtime



Additional best-practice responsibilities:

* Conduct quarterly platform health checks and provide performance reports

* Collaborate with marketing and sales teams to align automations with campaign objectives

* Research and pilot emerging automation tools and techniques



Qualifications

* Experience: 3+ years in marketing automation or system integration roles, with demonstrated expertise on GoHighLevel

* Technical Skills:

* Advanced proficiency with n8n and 
 for workflow automation

* Strong API development/integration experience (RESTful services, webhooks)

* Solid understanding of domain DNS, SSL, and SMS/A2P registration processes

* Soft Skills: Excellent problem-solving, clear written and verbal English communication, and strong documentation practices

* Tools: Comfortable using Hubstaff (or equivalent), Slack/Teams, and project-management platforms

title: HR Manager
description:

Location: Remote (Work from Home)
